The act(...) warnings in existing tests ("An update to ReconcilePage inside a test was not wrapped in act") come from async state updates in useCurrentPlan's useQuery. These are pre-existing warnings (not new to this step) and don't cause test failures.

useAutoReconcileAssist has eslint-disable on the exhaustive-deps for aiApi — this is intentional and documented in the comment, but future maintainers should know aiApi is stable (memoised by authToken in useAiApi).

`npm run lint -w frontend` still reports 12 pre-existing warnings unrelated to this step, but no lint errors.

The inline `vi.mocked(useAiApi).mockReturnValue(...)` constructs a partial object cast as the full `AiApi` type. Every new method added to `createAiApi` will require updating this mock manually. Consider using `vi.fn() as unknown as AiApi` with a partial to reduce future maintenance.

The `AiErrorBoundary` class is defined twice — once earlier in the file and the `AiCommitComposer` is wrapped in it, but the class definition exists as a copy from before. This is pre-existing across MyWeek.tsx. Not a bug since both components have their own local error boundaries.

The composer always shows an empty Phase 1 when re-opened (freeformText state resets). If the user typed substantial text and dismisses then re-opens, they lose it. Low priority for v1 but worth noting for UX polish.

The `useAutoReconcileAssist` and `useManagerAiSummary` exports were added to the aiHooks mock but are not actually called in MyWeek.tsx — they may be called in other components that are mocked away. No impact on correctness.
